purpose: Slice-3a measurement SUBSTRATE: a frozen, council-INDEPENDENT review-catch corpus. Code diffs each carry a seeded-defect oracle (catch), a clean-control region (cry-wolf), and a recall/no-narrowing control. The shared scorer (config/claude/metrics/council_review_scorer.py) consumes a reviewer flag-set + this oracle and scores BOTH metric families deterministically (no judge). 3a produces NO measurement number — that is Slice 3b.
matching_rule: OQ-DM-7: deterministic FILE + LINE-RANGE OVERLAP. A flag matches a defect iff SAME file AND the flag's [line_start..line_end] (or single line) intersects the defect's [line_start..line_end] INCLUSIVE on the endpoints (a touch counts); adjacent-non-touch is a miss; wrong file is a miss; one flag matches at most one defect.
provenance: Defects were seeded BEFORE and INDEPENDENT of any review (NGOAL-DM-003 Goodhart tripwire): each defect was planted at authoring time from a known anti-pattern, NOT selected because a model caught or missed it. The corpus is FROZEN and version-stamped (corpus_id + content hash over oracle.json + diffs/) and is NEVER re-selected or tuned per a measurement result.
variance_note: REAL across-task variance (BLOCKER-2): the tasks carry DISTINCT defect counts (T1-auth-token = 1 seeded defect; T2-pagination = 2), so under a fixed reviewer policy the per-task catch outcomes differ (>=2 distinct outcomes). This is NOT a single saturated task; it lets Slice 3b pin a noise threshold + MDE. 'Underpowered -> unmeasurable' is recorded as DISTINCT from 'refuted' in 3b.
goodhart_tripwire: NGOAL-DM-003: this corpus is authored INDEPENDENTLY of the council and NEVER tuned to flatter any arm. If a future change selects/drops diffs because of a model's catch/miss profile, the corpus is compromised and the measurement is Goodharted — re-freeze from independent provenance instead.
